beats.narration: Level one recognized impedance-plane patterns. Level two predicts them — because every variable pushes the dot its own way.

payload.title: READING THE IMPEDANCE PLANE
payload.sub: Predict the trajectory before the probe ever lands.

beats.narration: Lift-off chords toward the air point. Conductivity and frequency share one path down the comma curve. Permeability climbs above. Each direction is a fingerprint.

payload.heading: Every variable owns a direction
payload.note: LIFT-OFF → AIR POINT · σ & f SHARE THE CURVE · μ CLIMBS ABOVE

beats.narration: Phase is your depth ruler. A buried flaw answers rotated clockwise — about fifty-seven degrees per standard depth — and weaker, decaying to thirty-seven percent at one.

payload.heading: Rotation reads depth, amplitude reads severity

beats.narration: And the probe rewrites the picture. An absolute coil draws one excursion and sees every drift. A differential pair draws opposed lobes and cancels it.
So decompose before you call it. Every real trace is a vector sum — Level two is the discipline of naming each part.

payload.sub: The dot never lies — it answers in vector sums.
